title: The Spy Who Learned Me

jokes.text: Argh! D'oh!
jokes.setup: Episode opening with Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.punchline: The classic Homer 'D'oh!' response to some mishap
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - high character integration but low originality since it's his standard response

jokes.text: Now that's what I call going for a spin!
jokes.setup: Action sequence in spy movie with spinning element
jokes.punchline: Homer's overly literal commentary on the action
jokes.explanation: Basic Homer movie commentary - in character but predictable

jokes.text: Now that's what I call a snappy retort! / Stop saying what you call things! I'm trying to watch the movie!
jokes.setup: Homer continues his annoying commentary pattern
jokes.punchline: Marge's exasperated direct complaint about the pattern
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's annoying habit and Marge's realistic response

jokes.text: Yes, he was the perfect lover... of being killed! It's a weird sentence, but let's move on.
jokes.setup: Villain trying to sound menacing about killing spy
jokes.punchline: Awkward phrasing acknowledged by another character
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about awkward dialogue construction in spy movies

jokes.text: Hey, Homer, great yell-outs tonight! Lucky you, Marge! You get to hear 'em the loudest!
jokes.punchline: Stranger sarcastically 'complimenting' Marge on being closest to the annoyance
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic observation about marriage and public embarrassment

jokes.text: You've ruined me for other men! / That's my hobby. This is my job.
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact categorization of ruining women as hobby vs work
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic from Homer - the casual distinction between hobby and profession is perfectly unexpected

jokes.text: Looks like that guy's got turret's syndrome!
jokes.setup: Spy movie with action in towers/turrets
jokes.punchline: Homer confusing architectural turrets with Tourette's syndrome
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that fits Homer's tendency toward malapropisms

jokes.text: I couldn't have thought of that in 10,000 lifetimes!
jokes.setup: Homer's appreciation for his own joke
jokes.punchline: Absurdly long timeframe for not thinking of obvious pun
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-congratulation with absurd timeframe is mildly funny

jokes.text: This date night was even worse than the date night we saw Date Night.
jokes.punchline: Recursive reference to the movie 'Date Night' during their date night
jokes.explanation: Clever recursive wordplay that's economical and topical

jokes.text: Swiss cheese... Marge, how come you're not saying anything?
jokes.setup: Marge's obvious anger and silent treatment
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious question about her obvious anger
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness to social cues - well-executed character comedy

jokes.text: Oh man, Marge is pissed. Look at her... flaring her nostrils... drumming her fingers... making small noises like she's yelling at me inside her head...
jokes.punchline: Homer's detailed catalog of anger signs, especially the internal yelling
jokes.explanation: Great observational comedy about marriage - Homer's detailed awareness of Marge's anger patterns is both funny and realistic

jokes.text: That's what she said! / Another good one! You're on fire!
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriate 'That's what she said' and stranger's encouragement
jokes.explanation: Overused joke format but the stranger's encouragement adds slight twist

jokes.text: I should've taken that superintendent job in Honduras. My own donkey, all the sugarcane I could suck... if only I could stomach the upside-down question marks.
jokes.punchline: Escalating from reasonable regrets to absurd complaint about Spanish punctuation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity - starts with reasonable job regret, escalates to donkey ownership, peaks with punctuation xenophobia. Perfect Skinner neurosis.

jokes.text: Hey Simpson. Let me save you the trouble of putting that money back in your pocket. Haw-haw!
jokes.punchline: Nelson's 'helpful' offer to save Bart the trouble of keeping his money
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific bullying joke with mock politeness

jokes.text: My mom says bullies only bully 'cause they're scared. / Your mom's next!
jokes.punchline: Nelson threatening Bart's mom for the analysis
jokes.explanation: Sharp escalation - Nelson responding to psychological profiling by threatening the profiler's mother

jokes.text: You make dumb kids, lady.
jokes.punchline: Nelson's blunt assessment of Marge's parenting skills
jokes.explanation: Economical character comedy - Nelson's direct, crude way of insulting parenting

jokes.text: Trouble in paradise? / No, just my marriage.
jokes.setup: Lenny's optimistic question about Homer's relationship
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact distinction between paradise and marriage
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and structure - the contrast between 'paradise' and 'just my marriage' is devastatingly funny and relatable

jokes.text: Left for left, right for right! / Enough of your double-talk!
jokes.punchline: Homer finding the simplest possible instructions confusing
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's confusion at the simplest concepts

jokes.text: When I was in Africa, I had my skull cracked open by cannibals, and I'm still kicking! / Sir, that was your partner. You betrayed him to the cannibals. / Oh, that's right. I have his memories because I ate his brain.
jokes.setup: Burns claiming to have survived cannibals
jokes.punchline: Revelation that he betrayed his partner and ate the partner's brain for memories
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark escalation perfectly suited to Burns' evil character - the casual revelation of betrayal and cannibalism is both shocking and funny

jokes.text: But you said it so mean.
jokes.setup: Burns giving Homer paid time off
jokes.punchline: Homer's childlike complaint about Burns' delivery despite getting good news
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's childish focus on tone rather than content

jokes.text: Sharp. Flat. Sharp. Flat. / No one will tell me if I'm a ghost or not!
jokes.setup: Lisa tuning saxophone with alternating notes
jokes.punchline: Bart's random existential crisis about being dead
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor - Bart's random ghost anxiety amid mundane saxophone tuning

jokes.text: Got what, Homer? / Uh... I got... Shelbyville radio in my car for a few minutes.
jokes.setup: Homer about to reveal his vacation secret
jokes.punchline: Homer's weak improvised lie about radio reception
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection showing Homer's bad lying skills

jokes.text: Homer, I'm a single mother trying to raise a family here. / But you're not... / Just zip it!
jokes.setup: Marge claiming to be a single mother
jokes.punchline: Homer trying to correct the obvious error before being shut down
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment - Marge's dramatic misstatement and Homer's attempted correction

jokes.text: We could visit all our old romantic haunts that have closed and turned into temporary Halloween stores.
jokes.punchline: All their romantic places have become seasonal Halloween stores
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor about economic decline and the ubiquity of temporary Halloween stores - very relatable and specific

jokes.text: I didn't get much sleep last night, because I was up late again, lamenting the choices I've made. / Haw-haw! You can't change the past.
jokes.punchline: Nelson's cruel but accurate philosophical observation
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy - Skinner's oversharing met with Nelson's harsh wisdom

jokes.text: Do you want lies with that?
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'Do you want fries with that?' substituting 'lies'
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay parody of famous fast food phrase

jokes.text: That the town I sponsor in Africa doesn't exist? Prove it! Film all of Africa!
jokes.punchline: Demanding someone film entire continent to disprove him
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and absurdist logic - demanding impossible proof

jokes.text: You can just scrape it right off.
jokes.punchline: Cholesterol is so bad it's visible and scrapeable
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out visual humor about extreme cholesterol levels

jokes.text: If I don't have some kind of nugget every five minutes, I'll die.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific timing requirement for nuggets to survive
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of fast food addiction to absurd medical necessity

jokes.text: Hey, Homer. I could hear your pathetic rationalizing through the door.
jokes.punchline: Moe's brutal assessment of Homer's excuse before hearing it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Moe's cynical familiarity with Homer's patterns

jokes.text: Look, I'd love to discuss your problems, but a pack of raccoons took over my back room and today's the day I make my stand.
jokes.punchline: Moe has bigger problems involving raccoon occupation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation - raccoon takeover is perfectly absurd bar problem

jokes.text: I wish I could pull down my zipper and that guy would come out.
jokes.setup: Homer watching Stradivarius Cain in movie
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre wish to literally contain the spy inside himself
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist Homer logic about literally containing fictional character

jokes.text: Stranger things have happened, Homer.
jokes.setup: Homer's bizarre wish about unzipping to release spy
jokes.punchline: Moe's matter-of-fact acceptance that weirder things happen
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response - Moe's acceptance of absurdity as normal

jokes.text: Hello, Hufnpuffalus. / Sorry I slept with your girlfriend.
jokes.punchline: Casual apology for sleeping with imaginary girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about imaginary friend relationships and betrayals

jokes.text: Nelson, don't you want my lunch money? / Nah. Chicks wind up getting all your money anyway.
jokes.setup: Lisa offering lunch money to Nelson
jokes.punchline: Nelson's cynical philosophy about women and money
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor - Nelson displaying surprising adult cynicism about relationships

jokes.text: See if you can find out where my dolls are. / You might not like the answer. / I just want closure.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking Nelson about her missing dolls
jokes.punchline: Nelson's ominous warning followed by Lisa's mature acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy - Lisa's mature desire for closure about presumably destroyed dolls

jokes.text: Look at all them burgers and fries. I didn't know they served these things warm!
jokes.setup: Bart giving Nelson actual lunch instead of money
jokes.punchline: Nelson's amazement that food can be served warm
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Nelson's deprivation - not knowing food is served warm

jokes.text: Aw... they feel like a baby's head.
jokes.punchline: Disturbing comparison to baby's head
jokes.explanation: Mildly disturbing comparison that's more weird than funny

jokes.text: No, you give them the coupon, and they give you the hamburger. / I knew that.
jokes.punchline: Nelson's embarrassed claim he already knew
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Nelson's pride despite not understanding coupons

jokes.text: Free hamburger?! No, you give them the coupon, and they give you the hamburger. I knew that.
jokes.punchline: Nelson's embarrassment at not understanding how coupons work
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Nelson's unfamiliarity with basic commerce

jokes.text: Sometimes Marge says I turn it into a trashcan.
jokes.setup: Cain asking if Homer's car transforms
jokes.punchline: Homer's mundane answer about messy car habits
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of spy car tropes with realistic marital complaint

jokes.text: Sounds like you're turning into wife number two. I can do that. / So, stick with Marge!
jokes.setup: Homer interpreting advice as encouragement for affair
jokes.punchline: Cain's panicked correction about staying faithful
jokes.explanation: Good misunderstanding comedy - Homer's wrong interpretation needs correction

jokes.text: Childhood obesity... it's not funny anymore!
jokes.punchline: Claiming childhood obesity isn't funny while being in a comedy show
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about comedy show taking serious stance on issue it mocks

jokes.text: Breakfast cereals where the surprise inside is diabetes
jokes.punchline: Subverting toy prizes with disease diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - subverting childhood cereal prize expectations with serious health consequences

jokes.text: My dear, did you just fall from heaven? 'Cause your hair looks really messed up.
jokes.punchline: Subverting romantic compliment with insult about messy hair
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of classic pickup line - perfect misdirection from romantic to insulting

jokes.text: Okay, it matters a little what you say.
jokes.punchline: Cain's understated correction of his earlier advice
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to Homer's failure - understated correction

jokes.text: You know what wood makes the best ventriloquist dummy? Maple.
jokes.setup: Homer continuing to try pickup lines
jokes.punchline: Completely random fact about ventriloquist dummies
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist non-sequitur - completely inappropriate topic for seduction

jokes.text: Oh... I am a sucker for articulate fatsos.
jokes.setup: Homer delivering eloquent speech Cain taught him
jokes.punchline: Woman's specific attraction to well-spoken overweight men
jokes.explanation: Good specificity in character attraction - 'articulate fatsos' is funny and specific

jokes.text: I'm not talking about those pilots that hot dog in an air show while the real men are in combat.
jokes.punchline: Homer's specific dig at Blue Angels pilots vs combat pilots
jokes.explanation: Decent military humor showing Homer's unexpected specific knowledge

jokes.text: I thought I was making small talk. But it turned out to be big talk.
jokes.setup: Homer confused about why Marge is upset
jokes.punchline: Homer's distinction between small talk and big talk
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showing Homer's confusion about social interaction impact

jokes.text: Do you actually serve those items? No.
jokes.setup: Krusty claiming to offer healthy options
jokes.punchline: Krusty's blunt admission that they don't actually serve them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty moment - his casual admission of deception with no shame

jokes.text: Hugh Jackman Wolverine? / I'm not made of money. / Alicia Silverstone Batgirl? / He's that now.
jokes.punchline: Krusty's cheap alternatives ending with current terrible Batgirl
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of cheap alternatives and pop culture reference

jokes.text: Did you get a lot of work done, workerman?
jokes.setup: Marge suspecting Homer hasn't been working
jokes.punchline: Marge's sarcastic 'workerman' title
jokes.explanation: Decent sarcastic nickname showing Marge's suspicion

jokes.text: Kiss her. Kiss her while I watch.
jokes.punchline: Cain's voyeuristic request to watch them kiss
jokes.explanation: Mildly creepy humor that's more uncomfortable than funny

jokes.text: I'm not getting flea bites on my ankles!
jokes.punchline: Revealing the car previously had fleas
jokes.explanation: Good revelation of how disgusting Homer's car was before cleaning

jokes.text: I have bug-bombed the car for m'lady.
jokes.punchline: Homer's formal 'm'lady' address for pest control
jokes.explanation: Decent contrast between romantic language and pest control

jokes.text: No. Not you. Big fan, but no. / Cleatus the football robot, you're my only hope!
jokes.punchline: Rejecting one character but accepting football robot as last hope
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation with specific Fox Sports reference

jokes.text: Big fan, but no. Cleatus the football robot, you're my only hope!
jokes.setup: Homer desperately seeking help from imaginary characters
jokes.punchline: Rejecting most characters but appealing to random Fox Sports robot
jokes.explanation: Moderately funny pop culture reference showing Homer's random knowledge base

jokes.text: No one likes you!
jokes.punchline: Cleatus acknowledging his own unpopularity
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about the universally disliked Fox Sports robot

jokes.text: This is why I always say 'mingle at parties.' It's how I met Greg Kinnear. / Can't friends talk about friends? / He is not your friend! / Then why did he say he'd call me when he comes to Bolivia?
jokes.punchline: Escalating from party advice to Greg Kinnear friendship to Bolivia plans
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity - from reasonable social advice to bizarre celebrity relationship claims

jokes.text: At the risk of inflaming Sister Fate, this bully-free period has been an Arcadian idyll!
jokes.punchline: Martin's overly eloquent description of peaceful period
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Martin's pretentious vocabulary perfectly in character

jokes.text: Here, here! Idyll! Ha-ha!
jokes.punchline: Nelson's return ending their celebration
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Nelson's return ruining the nerds' brief freedom

jokes.text: I administer with this nose.
jokes.setup: New bully arriving to replace Nelson
jokes.punchline: Bully claiming to use nose as administrative tool
jokes.explanation: Weak absurdist humor that doesn't quite land

jokes.text: This is a happy ending? / Well, he's tough on nerds, but easy on the eyes.
jokes.setup: Martin questioning if new bully is good outcome
jokes.punchline: Lisa's shallow assessment based on appearance
jokes.explanation: Decent subversion of Lisa's usual moral stance with shallow comment about attractive bully

tmdb_overview: Marge and Homer go on a date, but Marge is embarrassed when Homer talks during the movie; when Homer gets hurt and can't work, he tries to learn how to be a better husband from a man he believes is a movie star.
